# UC-19.2.28 · HCI Erasure Coding Rebuild Amplification and EC-vs-RF Capacity Tradeoff

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Capacity, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check whether each storage pool picked a space-saving erasure layout that needs more separate failure zones than the cluster truly has, and we estimate how heavy rebuild traffic could get after a disk fails. We also watch whether the ratio of raw space used to logical data drifts away from what that layout promises, so teams fix policy mistakes during planning instead of during a slow recovery.*

---

## Description

Models per-container and per-pool erasure schemes versus configured fault domains on HCI stacks, estimates read-path rebuild amplification (RWAF) after a disk failure, tracks physical-to-logical overhead drift for EC versus mirror RF choices, and highlights policies that would demand more failure zones than the cluster currently exposes before the next outage drives a long rebuild.

## Value

Erasure coding buys raw capacity efficiency but shifts failure work onto wider stripes and more network reads; a mismatch between EC geometry and real rack or node diversity is a standing invitation to hours-long rebuild storms that RF-only compliance checks will not see. This use case gives change boards a numeric story on capacity savings versus rebuild cost, points to the exact container or policy rows that violate domain minima, and keeps capacity drift visible when compression ratios move even if the EC toggle never flipped.

## Implementation

Stand up the hci index and HEC exporters described in Step 1, ship transforms.conf for hci_ec_policy_owner.csv, install Splunk_TA_nutanix and optional VMware vSphere Forwarder, then paste the multisearch SPL into a saved search on a six-hour cadence with critical-to-high alert routing and freeze-aware demotion.

## Evidence

Saved search ss_uc_19_2_28_hci_ec_rwaf_tradeoff with scheduled CSV or PDF export, version-controlled lookups/hci_ec_policy_owner.csv including approver git hash, and optional Skyline-derived calibration worksheet stored with the quarterly tabletop notes.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In lab, ingest vmware:vsan:storage_policy and vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity where ec_scheme resolves to EC-4+2 while fault_domain_count is four, hci_ec_policy_owner lists production ownership, and the saved search emits ec_misconfigured equals one with severity critical within one scheduled cycle.

### Negative scenario

Populate hci_ec_policy_owner.change_freeze_until in the future for the same cluster_id and container_id while ec_misconfigured is zero because fault_domain_count meets fault_domain_required; severity should remain info and must not escalate to critical solely from a cosmetic rwaf_estimated rounding delta.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites

Governance and capacity models for this use case live in Splunk and in the storage teams runbooks at the same time. Create a UTF-8 CSV at lookups/hci_ec_policy_owner.csv and publish it through transforms.conf as hci_ec_policy_owner so operators can type inputlookup hci_ec_policy_owner.csv in Search. Columns are cluster_id, vendor, container_id, owner_team, target_max_capacity_drift_pct, target_max_rwaf, ec_target_scheme, change_freeze_until. The cluster_id and container_id strings must match what your collectors emit after the coalesce normalisation in Step 2; if a HyperFlex row is cluster-wide, use a sentinel container_id such as hyperflex_cluster_storage with the same spelling in every layer. The vendor column is the normalised slug vmware, nutanix, cisco, or microsoft. owner_team is a mail-enabled group or triage queue name, not a person, so paging does not break on leave. target_max_capacity_drift_pct is a decimal percent threshold such as 5 meaning five percent relative drift against the expected overhead ratio for the declared scheme. target_max_rwaf is a floating cap for the estimated read-path rebuild amplification factor used as a secondary gate when the scheme is erasure-like. ec_target_scheme is the documented goal for that row such as RF2, RF3, EC-4+2, EC-4+1, MAP, or EC-single-parity, and is how you anchor expected overhead when the live telemetry label is noisy. change_freeze_until is an ISO-8601 instant or bare YYYY-MM-DD date; when now() is before that instant the alert logic demotes non-critical severities to info so an approved maintenance window does not spam the same channel as an unforeseen breach.

Indexes: keep a dedicated hci index when possible, or split per vendor vmware, nutanix, cisco, microsoft as in UC-19.2.6 and UC-19.2.16, but reserve hci_summary for acceleration jobs if you materialise hourly rollups. HEC tokens should segregate production and lab to avoid cross-pollution in lookups.

Sourcetype expectations, aligned with Step 1 collection design: vmware:vsan:storage_policy carries PowerCLI or vCenter automation exports of Get-SpbmStoragePolicy style fields including numberOfFailuresToTolerate, replicaPreference or failure_tolerance_method, proportionalCapacity, policy name and id, and cluster identifiers. vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity carries vsan_logical_used_gb, vsan_physical_used_gb, dedupe_compression_ratio, fault_domain_count, host_count as your poller maps them. vmware:vsan:component can supply component-level detail; for this UC it is optional enrichment when you need to reconcile logical versus physical without waiting for capacity summaries. nutanix:storage_pool and nutanix:container come from Splunk_TA_nutanix. nutanix:container:ec is the custom HEC stream from Prism Element REST /PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/storage_containers/?include=ec_strip_size,replication_factor,fingerprint_on_write,advertised_capacity_bytes,used_bytes,reserved_bytes so EC strip geometry is not flattened away. hyperflex:cluster:storage is the HX Connect REST /rest/clusters/{cid}/storage projection; treat replicationFactor as RF2 or RF3 only and document that Cisco HyperFlex does not expose VMware-style RAID-6 erasure policies on that JSON path, which is a vendor caveat for finance conversations, not a Splunk limitation. azure_stack_hci:storage_pool and azure_stack_hci:virtual_disk:resiliency are scheduled exports from Get-StoragePool and Get-VirtualDisk showing ResiliencySettingName, PhysicalDiskRedundancy, NumberOfDataCopies, NumberOfDataColumns, and pool identifiers.

Secondary streams: vmware:vc:event from Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder correlates administrator policy edits and storage-profile changes that explain sudden drifts. vSAN Skyline Health exports of Cluster Operation Resync Objects provide observed rebuild megabytes per second from historical incidents; feed a low-rate summary sourcetype if you want empirical tuning of warning thresholds, but keep the mathematic baseline in this UC so it still runs when Skyline is offline.

Tertiary collectors: Splunk_TA_nutanix ncli sidecars can capture ec_strip_size_bytes, ec_data_blocks, ec_parity_blocks when REST summary lacks them. Store them on the same container key as nutanix:container:ec to avoid double counting.

Vendor-specific minimum fault-domain rules used when you sanity-check Splunk output by hand: VMware vSAN RAID-5 erasure is commonly described as a four plus one scheme and requires at least four fault domains for that layout class in VMware documentation sets you rely on; RAID-6 four plus two requires at least six. Nutanix EC is stated as EC-X plus Y requiring at least X plus Y plus one fault domains in many field guides; this UC also evaluates the conservative fault_domain_required equals ec_data_blocks plus ec_parity_blocks plus one so a row surfaces even when marketing tables round differently. Cisco HyperFlex remains RF-only in the public API described here; do not infer parity blocks from replicationFactor alone. Azure Stack HCI parity and mirror-accelerated parity generally need at least four cluster nodes for the dual-parity class recipes Microsoft documents; mirror-only two-node clusters are out of scope for parity erasure in the exclusions list.

RWAF in plain terms: when a disk fails under mirroring with two full copies, rebuilding that disk mostly reads one surviving full copy once per address range, which is why this UC sets rwaf_estimated to 1.0 for RF-labelled schemes. Under a wide stripe such as four data plus two parity, the rebuild task must touch multiple surviving strips per stripe; a textbook read amplification for the rebuild path is the count of all strips participating in the stripe divided by the count of data strips, for example six divided by four equals one point five. Network traffic amplifies further when strips are not co-resident on the same node, which is why you pair this UC with topology context from UC-19.2.16 rather than pretending a single scalar captures fabric behaviour.

Post-failure spare-capacity hit is approximated by comparing used_physical_gb to used_logical_gb through capacity_overhead_actual_ratio, then tracking relative drift versus the ratio implied by ec_target_scheme or by the live ec_scheme. This is not a substitute for vendor slack-space algorithms in UC-19.2.27; it is a policy and amplification guardrail.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection

VMware vSphere and vSAN: schedule PowerCLI on a management host with a read-only automation role to run Get-SpbmStoragePolicy against every vSAN-aware policy object you attach to datastores or VMs, exporting numberOfFailuresToTolerate, replicaPreference or subProfileFailureToleranceMethod, proportionalCapacity, and policy identifiers, then POST one JSON event per policy per cluster to HEC with sourcetype vmware:vsan:storage_policy. Separately, export cluster capacity summaries for vsan_logical_used_gb and vsan_physical_used_gb with dedupe_compression_ratio to vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity, including fault_domain_count or host_count as proxies until rack labels arrive. Optionally export aggregated Get-VsanComponent summaries to vmware:vsan:component when you need component-level reconciliation. In props.conf on the first parsing tier set SHOULD_LINEMERGE false, TRUNCATE at least 1048576 for fat arrays, KV_MODE json or INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS json, TIME_PREFIX if the poller embeds time. inputs.conf should use a dedicated host and queue directory on the poller so a slow policy crawl does not block capacity snapshots.

Nutanix: deploy Splunk_TA_nutanix and enable nutanix:storage_pool and nutanix:container on a five to fifteen minute interval depending on change risk. Add a small Python poller on a management VM with HTTPS to Prism Element on 9440 that calls the storage_containers endpoint with include flags for ec_strip_size, replication_factor, advertised_capacity_bytes, used_bytes, reserved_bytes, and POSTs to HEC as nutanix:container:ec. Map used_bytes to used_physical_gb with divide by pow(1024,3) in SPL or in the poller for consistency. If you must stay agentless, schedule nutanix:container:ec less frequently but never slower than six hours on production because EC toggles are rare and consequential.

Cisco HyperFlex: poll HX Connect REST /rest/clusters/{cid}/storage on the same schedule as other HX inventory, emitting sourcetype hyperflex:cluster:storage with replicationFactor, capacity and free fields as exposed on your software generation, and static labels for cluster_name and cluster_id. Document in the app README that erasure coding as implemented on other stacks is not represented here, so ec_data_blocks stays one and ec_parity_blocks stays zero with ec_scheme RF2 or RF3 only.

Azure Stack HCI: schedule PowerShell on a cluster management node or orchestration server to run Get-StoragePool and Get-VirtualDisk, projecting FriendlyName or unique Id, ResiliencySettingName, PhysicalDiskRedundancy, NumberOfDataCopies, NumberOfDataColumns, HealthStatus, and Size. POST JSON to HEC as azure_stack_hci:storage_pool for pools and azure_stack_hci:virtual_disk:resiliency for volumes, carrying a shared cluster_id field.

Vendor to common schema mapping table used by the SPL normalisation: cluster_id is coalesce of vendor-specific cluster keys. container_id is policy name or uuid on VMware, container uuid or name on Nutanix, a datastore or cluster sentinel on Cisco, volume or pool friendly name on Azure. ec_scheme is a short label RF1, RF2, RF3, EC-4+1, EC-4+2, MAP, EC-single-parity, EC-dual-parity as derived. ec_data_blocks and ec_parity_blocks follow the erasure strip counts you export or infer; mirrors use one and zero. rf mirrors NumberOfDataCopies for Azure mirrors and replication_factor for Nutanix and HyperFlex, zero when the row is parity-first. fault_domain_count comes from vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity first, else a lookup backfill you join from UC-19.2.16 materialised summaries if needed. used_logical_gb and used_physical_gb come from vsan logical and physical on VMware, used and advertised ratios on Nutanix after conversion, usable or used bytes on HyperFlex, volume size and footprint fields on Azure depending on which cmdlet you trust for logical guest sizing.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL below as ss_uc_19_2_28_hci_ec_rwaf_tradeoff with permissions limited to storage platform and observability admins. Schedule every six hours on cron 0 star-slash-6 star star star star relative to the search head timezone, with earliest minus twenty-four hours at hour snapping and latest at hour to align exec summaries. Alert when severity is critical or high after the change-freeze demotion logic, route critical to your storage major incident queue, high to ServiceNow risk tasks, warning to Slack channel hci-ec-tradeoff, info retained for dashboards only. Throttle using vendor cluster_id container_id dedup keys for four hours on warning so strip-size firmware rollouts do not page repeatedly. Document that UC-19.2.6 remains the RF compliance source of truth for integer copy policy, UC-19.2.9 and UC-19.2.30 cover rebuild progress and vSAN resync depth respectively, and this UC is the EC policy correctness and EC versus RF capacity trade-off layer.

Alert rationale tied to customer outcomes: critical fires when fault_domain_count is below fault_domain_required for a geometry that needs more failure zones than you operate, which is the standing condition that turns a single disk replacement into a prolonged rebuild storm. Warning fires on capacity_drift_pct beyond target_max_capacity_drift_pct or the five percent default encoded in the lookup filler, which catches dedupe ratio swings masquerading as EC changes until you confirm against Skyline or Prism. High surfaces when rwaf_estimated exceeds target_max_rwaf on production-like erasure rows, meaning change review should rehearse rebuild bandwidth before approving additional erasure expansion.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.28 — HCI erasure coding rebuild amplification and EC-vs-RF capacity tradeoff; multisearch normalizes VMware Nutanix Cisco Microsoft; hci_ec_policy_owner lookup; RWAF read-path estimate; tunables earliest=-7d@h; owner storage-platform; change freeze demotes non-critical severities; EC model vSAN RAID-5/6 Nutanix EC-X+Y HyperFlex RF-only Azure parity/MAP.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h ( sourcetype="vmware:vsan:storage_policy" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:component" )
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, vsphere_cluster, vcenter_cluster)
      | eval container_id=case(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:storage_policy", coalesce(policy_id, policyId, policy_name, policyName, name, "vSAN_POLICY"), sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity", "vSAN_CLUSTER_CAPACITY", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:component", coalesce(component_cluster_key, cluster_id, "vSAN_COMPONENT"), 1=1, "vSAN_ROW")
      | eval vendor="vmware"
      | eval ftml=lower(tostring(coalesce(replica_preference, replicaPreference, failure_tolerance_method, failureToleranceMethod, ftm, "RAID-1")))
      | eval pftt=tonumber(coalesce(numberOfFailuresToTolerate, number_of_failures_to_tolerate, pftt, primary_failures_to_tolerate, PFTT, "1"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme=case(like(ftml,"%raid-6%") OR ftml="raid-6" OR like(ftml,"%erasure-6%"), "EC-4+2", like(ftml,"%raid-5%") OR ftml="raid-5" OR like(ftml,"%erasure-5%"), "EC-4+1", like(ftml,"%mirror%") OR like(ftml,"%raid-1%"), if(pftt>=2, "RF3", if(pftt>=1, "RF2", "RF1")), 1=1, "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 4, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 4, 1=1, 1)
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 2, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 1, 1=1, 0)
      | eval rf=case(ec_scheme="RF3", 3, ec_scheme="RF2", 2, ec_scheme="RF1", 1, 1=1, 0)
      | eval vsan_log=tonumber(coalesce(vsan_logical_used_gb, vsanLogicalUsedGB, logical_used_gb, logicalUsedGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval vsan_phys=tonumber(coalesce(vsan_physical_used_gb, vsanPhysicalUsedGB, physical_used_gb, physicalUsedGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, usedLogicalGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_physical_gb=tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, usedPhysicalGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=if(vsan_log>0, vsan_log, used_logical_gb)
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(vsan_phys>0, vsan_phys, used_physical_gb)
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, host_count, hostCount, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h ( sourcetype="nutanix:storage_pool" OR sourcetype="nutanix:container" OR sourcetype="nutanix:container:ec" )
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, container_uuid, containerUuid, container_name, containerName, storage_pool_uuid, storagePoolUuid, storage_pool, storagePool, name, "NUTANIX_CONTAINER")
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | eval rf_raw=tonumber(coalesce(replication_factor, replicationFactor, replication_factor_num, "2"), 10)
      | eval db_raw=tonumber(coalesce(ec_data_blocks, ecDataBlocks, ec_data_fragments, "0"), 10)
      | eval pb_raw=tonumber(coalesce(ec_parity_blocks, ecParityBlocks, ec_parity_fragments, "0"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme_hint=lower(tostring(coalesce(ec_scheme, ecScheme, ec_profile, erasure_coding_policy, "")))
      | eval ec_scheme=case(db_raw>=6 AND pb_raw>=2, "EC-6+2", db_raw>=4 AND pb_raw>=2, "EC-4+2", db_raw>=4 AND pb_raw=1, "EC-4+1", db_raw>=3 AND pb_raw=1, "EC-3+1", db_raw>=2 AND pb_raw=1, "EC-2+1", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%4+2%"), "EC-4+2", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%4+1%"), "EC-4+1", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%3+1%"), "EC-3+1", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%2+1%"), "EC-2+1", rf_raw=3, "RF3", 1=1, "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-6+2", 6, ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 4, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 4, ec_scheme="EC-3+1", 3, ec_scheme="EC-2+1", 2, 1=1, 1)
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-6+2", 2, ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 2, ec_scheme="EC-4+1" OR ec_scheme="EC-3+1" OR ec_scheme="EC-2+1", 1, 1=1, 0)
      | eval rf=if(ec_scheme="RF3", 3, if(ec_scheme="RF2", rf_raw, 0))
      | eval used_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(used_bytes, UsedBytes, used_bytes_total, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, usedLogicalGB, advertised_capacity_gb, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(used_bytes>0, used_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, usedPhysicalGB, "0"), 10))
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, rack_fault_domain_count, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:storage"
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, clusterUuid, cluster_uuid, hx_cluster_id)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, datastore_name, datastoreName, "hyperflex_cluster_storage")
      | eval vendor="cisco"
      | eval rf=tonumber(coalesce(replicationFactor, replication_factor, NumberOfDataCopies, "2"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme=if(rf=3, "RF3", "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=1
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=0
      | eval used_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(used_bytes, UsedBytes, used_capacity_bytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval total_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(total_bytes, TotalBytes, total_capacity_bytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=if(total_bytes>0 AND used_bytes>=0, total_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, "0"), 10))
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(used_bytes>0, used_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, "0"), 10))
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, node_count, nodeCount, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h ( sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:storage_pool" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:virtual_disk:resiliency" )
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, hci_cluster)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, FriendlyName, friendlyName, virtual_disk_name, VirtualDiskName, storage_pool_name, Name, "azure_hci_volume")
      | eval vendor="microsoft"
      | eval rsn=lower(tostring(coalesce(ResiliencySettingName, resiliency_setting_name, "Mirror")))
      | eval ndc=tonumber(coalesce(NumberOfDataCopies, number_of_data_copies, "2"), 10)
      | eval pdr=tonumber(coalesce(PhysicalDiskRedundancy, physical_disk_redundancy, "0"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme=case(like(rsn,"%dual%parity%"), "EC-dual-parity", (like(rsn,"%single%parity%") OR (like(rsn,"%parity%") AND !like(rsn,"%mirror%") AND !like(rsn,"%map%"))), "EC-single-parity", like(rsn,"%mirror%accelerated%parity%") OR like(rsn,"%map%"), "MAP", like(rsn,"%mirror%"), if(ndc>=3, "RF3", "RF2"), 1=1, "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-dual-parity", pdr+2, ec_scheme="EC-single-parity", pdr+1, ec_scheme="MAP", 4, 1=1, 1)
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-dual-parity", 2, ec_scheme="EC-single-parity", 1, ec_scheme="MAP", 2, 1=1, 0)
      | eval rf=if(ec_scheme="RF3" OR ec_scheme="RF2", ndc, 0)
      | eval size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(Size, size_bytes, PoolSizeBytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval alloc_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(AllocatedSize, allocated_size_bytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=if(size_bytes>0, size_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, "0"), 10))
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(alloc_bytes>0, alloc_bytes/pow(1024,3), if(size_bytes>0, size_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, "0"), 10)))
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, OperationalNodes, node_count, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
| eval cluster_id=if(isnotnull(cluster_id) AND len(tostring(cluster_id))>0, tostring(cluster_id), "UNKNOWN")
| eval container_id=if(isnotnull(container_id) AND len(tostring(container_id))>0, tostring(container_id), "UNKNOWN")
| where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN" AND container_id!="UNKNOWN" AND ( vendor="vmware" OR vendor="nutanix" OR vendor="cisco" OR vendor="microsoft" )
| eval capacity_overhead_expected_ratio=case(ec_scheme="RF1", 1.0, ec_scheme="RF2", 2.0, ec_scheme="RF3", 3.0, ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 1.5, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 1.25, ec_scheme="EC-3+1", 4.0/3.0, ec_scheme="EC-2+1", 1.5, ec_scheme="EC-6+2", 8.0/6.0, ec_scheme="EC-single-parity", (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, ec_scheme="EC-dual-parity", (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, ec_scheme="MAP", (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1=1, if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 2.0))
| eval capacity_overhead_actual_ratio=if(used_logical_gb>0, used_physical_gb/used_logical_gb, null())
| eval capacity_drift_pct=if(isnotnull(capacity_overhead_expected_ratio) AND capacity_overhead_expected_ratio>0 AND isnotnull(capacity_overhead_actual_ratio), 100*((capacity_overhead_actual_ratio-capacity_overhead_expected_ratio)/capacity_overhead_expected_ratio), null())
| eventstats max(fault_domain_count) as fdom_peak by cluster_id, vendor, _time
| eval fault_domain_count=coalesce(fault_domain_count, fdom_peak)
| sort 0 cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time
| streamstats window=168 global=f avg(capacity_drift_pct) as capacity_drift_pct_ma7 by cluster_id, container_id, vendor
| stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count latest(capacity_overhead_actual_ratio) as capacity_overhead_actual_ratio_join latest(capacity_overhead_expected_ratio) as capacity_overhead_expected_ratio_forjoin latest(capacity_drift_pct_ma7) as capacity_drift_pct max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id, vendor, container_id
    [| inputlookup hci_ec_policy_owner.csv
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, CLUSTER_ID, cluster)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, CONTAINER_ID, container)
      | eval vendor=lower(tostring(vendor))
      | fields cluster_id, vendor, container_id, owner_team, target_max_capacity_drift_pct, target_max_rwaf, ec_target_scheme, change_freeze_until ]
| eval scheme_tgt=coalesce(ec_target_scheme, ec_scheme)
| eval capacity_overhead_expected_ratio=case(scheme_tgt="RF1", 1.0, scheme_tgt="RF2", 2.0, scheme_tgt="RF3", 3.0, scheme_tgt="EC-4+2", 1.5, scheme_tgt="EC-4+1", 1.25, scheme_tgt="EC-3+1", 4.0/3.0, scheme_tgt="EC-2+1", 1.5, scheme_tgt="EC-6+2", 8.0/6.0, scheme_tgt="EC-single-parity", if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1.25), scheme_tgt="EC-dual-parity", if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1.5), scheme_tgt="MAP", if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1.5), 1=1, capacity_overhead_expected_ratio_forjoin)
| eval capacity_drift_pct=if(isnotnull(capacity_overhead_actual_ratio_join) AND isnotnull(capacity_overhead_expected_ratio) AND capacity_overhead_expected_ratio>0, 100*((capacity_overhead_actual_ratio_join-capacity_overhead_expected_ratio)/capacity_overhead_expected_ratio), capacity_drift_pct)
| fillnull value=5 target_max_capacity_drift_pct
| fillnull value=2.25 target_max_rwaf
| eval target_max_capacity_drift_pct=tonumber(target_max_capacity_drift_pct, 10)
| eval target_max_rwaf=tonumber(target_max_rwaf, 10)
| eval fault_domain_required=ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks+1
| eval ec_misconfigured=if(fault_domain_count<fault_domain_required, 1, 0)
| eval ecdb=if(ec_data_blocks<1, 1, ec_data_blocks)
| eval rwaf_estimated=if(ec_scheme="RF2" OR ec_scheme="RF3" OR ec_scheme="RF1", 1.0, (ecdb+ec_parity_blocks)/ecdb)
| eval sev0=case(ec_misconfigured=1, "critical", rwaf_estimated>target_max_rwaf AND ec_parity_blocks>0, "high", ( abs(capacity_drift_pct)>target_max_capacity_drift_pct AND isnotnull(capacity_drift_pct) ), "warning", ec_misconfigured=0 AND rwaf_estimated<=target_max_rwaf AND ( isnull(capacity_drift_pct) OR abs(capacity_drift_pct)<=target_max_capacity_drift_pct ), "info", rwaf_estimated>target_max_rwaf, "high", 1=1, "warning")
| eval cft1=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(cft1) OR isnotnull(cft2) OR isnotnull(cft3) OR isnotnull(cft4), if(now()<coalesce(cft1,cft2,cft3,cft4,0),1,0),0)
| eval severity=if(freeze_active=1 AND sev0!="critical", "info", sev0)
| sort 0 - ec_misconfigured, - rwaf_estimated, cluster_id, container_id
| table vendor, cluster_id, container_id, ec_scheme, fault_domain_count, fault_domain_required, rwaf_estimated, capacity_drift_pct, severity, owner_team, change_freeze_until
```

### Step 3 — Validate

Validation is intentionally about arithmetic traceability rather than repeating RF-only compliance checks from UC-19.2.6. For VMware, open Menu then Policies and Profiles then VM Storage Policies, edit the policy named in container_id, and read the failure to tolerate and RAID level fields beside numberOfFailuresToTolerate and replicaPreference in the export. Confirm the Splunk ec_scheme label you derive matches that editor for RAID-5 versus RAID-6 versus RAID-1. For Nutanix, Prism Element Storage, Storage Container, Update, Container Properties, Erasure Coding should show whether EC is enabled and which EC profile is active; compare ec_data_blocks and ec_parity_blocks in Splunk to that panel, not only replication factor. For Azure Stack HCI, Windows Admin Center Cluster Storage Volumes Properties Resiliency should match ResiliencySettingName in azure_stack_hci:virtual_disk:resiliency events.

Hand-calculate RWAF once per quarter as a tabletop exercise: pick EC-4+2, write six divided by four equals one point five, compare to rwaf_estimated in the search result for the same container_id. If they differ, your ec_data_blocks telemetry is wrong, not the maths in the search. Confirm fault_domain_required equals ec_data_blocks plus ec_parity_blocks plus one on a whiteboard with storage architects and compare to fault_domain_count from vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity or your rack-aware counter from UC-19.2.16. For Cisco rows, validate replicationFactor is two or three only and acknowledge the HyperFlex EC caveat aloud in the meeting notes so finance does not compare Cisco numbers to vSAN RAID-6 rows.

Correlation checks: when vmware:vc:event shows a profile edit at the same hour as a drift spike, treat capacity_drift_pct as expected transient until the 168-hour moving average from streamstats clears. When only dedupe_compression_ratio moves, follow UC-19.2.27 style slack review before calling the EC misconfiguration runbook.

### Step 4 — Operationalise

Dashboard layout: first row single value of ec_misconfigured count for production clusters, second row scorecard of ec_scheme versus fault_domain_required minus fault_domain_count gap per cluster_id, third row table of rwaf_estimated sorted descending for erasure rows only, fourth row scatter with capacity savings on one axis as one minus one divided by capacity_overhead_actual_ratio and rebuild cost proxy on the other axis as rwaf_estimated times a nominal failure probability scalar you calibrate from Skyline history. Add a drilldown from any point to the saved search with tokens for vendor and cluster_id.

Runbook headings: when to migrate from RF equals two to EC-4+2 requires documenting minimum node count, observed write latency SLO, and acceptance of higher rebuild traffic; when EC is contraindicated lists write-heavy databases and two-rack layouts without spare domains; EC policy change communication checklist includes storage owner, backup owner, DBA where applicable, network owner because east-west rebuild traffic rises, and CAB references. Ownership stays with Head of Platform or delegated storage SRE on follow-the-sun handoffs.

Tie-ins: UC-19.2.6 proves whether the replication factor and method on the tin match the written standard; this UC asks whether erasure geometry matches the fault domains you truly run and quantifies amplification. UC-19.2.9 and UC-19.2.30 tell you how long rebuilds take in motion; this UC estimates how much work a failure will ask for before motion starts. UC-19.2.16 provides rack and block topology context so fault_domain_count is not only host count. UC-19.2.27 covers slack formulas that should be consulted when capacity_drift_pct moves but ec_scheme did not.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

Case cluster expansion in progress: fault_domain_count temporarily lags newly added nodes until vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity and UC-19.2.16 topology catch up; extend change_freeze_until through balance jobs and confirm a second poll before critical pages.

Case multiple vSAN policies per cluster: incorrect grouping by cluster alone will blend RAID-1 VMs with RAID-6 VMs; require container_id to carry policy id or policy name and never aggregate dissimilar policies. If you see impossible rwaf_estimated on a mixed policy export, split the poller.

Case Nutanix Karbon or RDMA specialised containers: EC may be enabled only on some container UUIDs; join business_service from CMDB so low-touch Kubernetes volumes do not inherit production EC expectations. If ec_strip_size flips after AOS upgrade, compare ncli output to nutanix:container:ec before opening a vendor ticket.

Case Azure mirror-accelerated parity: rebuild initially hits mirror tiers then parity tiers; rwaf_estimated from column counts is an upper-bound proxy, not a byte-for-byte predictor. Pair with Storage Reliability Counter samples if you extended UC-19.2.30 style feeds.

Case firmware or on-disk format upgrade: default strip sizes can change without an admin edit; if ec_scheme stable but capacity_drift_pct rises, inspect dedupe and compression ratios first.

Case dedupe ratio drift: capacity_drift_pct may exceed five percent while ec_scheme unchanged; confirm with vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity dedupe_compression_ratio before reclassifying as EC breach.

Case HyperFlex JSON rename: if replicationFactor moves under a nested path, widen coalesce lists rather than muting the alert.

Case streamstats sparsity: when fewer than one hundred sixty-eight hourly samples exist after greenfield rollout, capacity_drift_pct_ma7 equals short-history average; document in the dashboard subtitle until thirty days of history accrue.

Long-form engineering notes for audits: retain HEC listener access logs with hash chains per your SOC policy. Keep hci_ec_policy_owner.csv in git with pull requests and CODEOWNERS on the storage guild. Re-run fieldsummary after every major TA upgrade across all named sourcetypes. Where Legal requires data residency, run an on-prem search head with the same SPL and export PDF evidence packs rather than shipping raw volumes cross-border. When ITSI is present, mirror severity critical as a transient KPI dip on a storage service entity keyed by cluster_id. Where ServiceNow is authoritative for change_freeze_until, automate a scripted REST output that overwrites the CSV nightly to avoid stale freezes. For dual-control environments, require two human approvals to change ec_target_scheme on a production row. When Splunk Cloud Victoria Experience limits join fanout, pre-materialise the multisearch arms as summary indexes on a fifteen minute slide. For air-gapped sites, bundle the Python poller inside the same signed package as your other HCI tools. When observability budget is tight, run this search daily instead of every six hours but widen earliest to minus forty-eight hours to avoid missing short stripes. When vendor professional services rename clusters during divestiture, batch-update cluster_id in hci_ec_policy_owner.csv the same minute DNS changes propagate. When legal discovery requests historic Splunk results, export this saved search with its version id and the lookup git commit hash together. When training interns, use lab Nutanix EC-2 plus one on four nodes intentionally mis-set to three domains to show a critical row without touching production.

Operational review cadence: monthly readout of rwaf_estimated quantiles by vendor for CAB; quarterly cross-check with finance on capacity savings from EC-4 plus 2 versus mirror RF2 using capacity_overhead_actual_ratio trends; annual tabletop that assumes simultaneous disk and switch failure to stress fault_domain_required maths.

Handover checklist for new platform owners: confirm hci_ec_policy_owner.csv columns, confirm HEC tokens, confirm time sync on pollers, confirm Skyline feed optional status, confirm alert routes, confirm runbook location, confirm UC cross references in internal wiki.

Supplement A: when a storage admin changes proportionalCapacity in vSAN without changing FT, expect capacity_drift_pct to move while RWAF stays flat; treat as finance review not topology review. Supplement B: when Nutanix enables compression only, overhead_actual_ratio falls without EC; annotate dashboards with a compression toggle legend. Supplement C: when Cisco publishes a new JSON field for future EC, branch a new sourcetype rather than overloading hyperflex:cluster:storage so historic rows stay comparable. Supplement D: when Azure switches a volume from mirror to dual parity, expect a discontinuous jump in overhead_expected_ratio; require a CMDB tag on container_id before and after. Supplement E: when Splunk ingest latency exceeds one hour, streamstats windows shift; monitor _indextime minus _time. Supplement F: when multiple HEC tokens write the same sourcetype, dedup on source host in props to preserve ordering. Supplement G: when search head clusters restart mid-query, saved-search idempotency relies on cron overlap tolerance you document in ops. Supplement H: when GDPR requests omit certain clusters, maintain parallel hci_ec_policy_owner files per region.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.28 — HCI erasure coding rebuild amplification and EC-vs-RF capacity tradeoff; multisearch normalizes VMware Nutanix Cisco Microsoft; hci_ec_policy_owner lookup; RWAF read-path estimate; tunables earliest=-7d@h; owner storage-platform; change freeze demotes non-critical severities; EC model vSAN RAID-5/6 Nutanix EC-X+Y HyperFlex RF-only Azure parity/MAP.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h ( sourcetype="vmware:vsan:storage_policy" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:component" )
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, vsphere_cluster, vcenter_cluster)
      | eval container_id=case(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:storage_policy", coalesce(policy_id, policyId, policy_name, policyName, name, "vSAN_POLICY"), sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:capacity", "vSAN_CLUSTER_CAPACITY", sourcetype="vmware:vsan:component", coalesce(component_cluster_key, cluster_id, "vSAN_COMPONENT"), 1=1, "vSAN_ROW")
      | eval vendor="vmware"
      | eval ftml=lower(tostring(coalesce(replica_preference, replicaPreference, failure_tolerance_method, failureToleranceMethod, ftm, "RAID-1")))
      | eval pftt=tonumber(coalesce(numberOfFailuresToTolerate, number_of_failures_to_tolerate, pftt, primary_failures_to_tolerate, PFTT, "1"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme=case(like(ftml,"%raid-6%") OR ftml="raid-6" OR like(ftml,"%erasure-6%"), "EC-4+2", like(ftml,"%raid-5%") OR ftml="raid-5" OR like(ftml,"%erasure-5%"), "EC-4+1", like(ftml,"%mirror%") OR like(ftml,"%raid-1%"), if(pftt>=2, "RF3", if(pftt>=1, "RF2", "RF1")), 1=1, "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 4, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 4, 1=1, 1)
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 2, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 1, 1=1, 0)
      | eval rf=case(ec_scheme="RF3", 3, ec_scheme="RF2", 2, ec_scheme="RF1", 1, 1=1, 0)
      | eval vsan_log=tonumber(coalesce(vsan_logical_used_gb, vsanLogicalUsedGB, logical_used_gb, logicalUsedGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval vsan_phys=tonumber(coalesce(vsan_physical_used_gb, vsanPhysicalUsedGB, physical_used_gb, physicalUsedGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, usedLogicalGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_physical_gb=tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, usedPhysicalGB, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=if(vsan_log>0, vsan_log, used_logical_gb)
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(vsan_phys>0, vsan_phys, used_physical_gb)
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, host_count, hostCount, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h ( sourcetype="nutanix:storage_pool" OR sourcetype="nutanix:container" OR sourcetype="nutanix:container:ec" )
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, container_uuid, containerUuid, container_name, containerName, storage_pool_uuid, storagePoolUuid, storage_pool, storagePool, name, "NUTANIX_CONTAINER")
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | eval rf_raw=tonumber(coalesce(replication_factor, replicationFactor, replication_factor_num, "2"), 10)
      | eval db_raw=tonumber(coalesce(ec_data_blocks, ecDataBlocks, ec_data_fragments, "0"), 10)
      | eval pb_raw=tonumber(coalesce(ec_parity_blocks, ecParityBlocks, ec_parity_fragments, "0"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme_hint=lower(tostring(coalesce(ec_scheme, ecScheme, ec_profile, erasure_coding_policy, "")))
      | eval ec_scheme=case(db_raw>=6 AND pb_raw>=2, "EC-6+2", db_raw>=4 AND pb_raw>=2, "EC-4+2", db_raw>=4 AND pb_raw=1, "EC-4+1", db_raw>=3 AND pb_raw=1, "EC-3+1", db_raw>=2 AND pb_raw=1, "EC-2+1", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%4+2%"), "EC-4+2", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%4+1%"), "EC-4+1", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%3+1%"), "EC-3+1", like(ec_scheme_hint,"%2+1%"), "EC-2+1", rf_raw=3, "RF3", 1=1, "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-6+2", 6, ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 4, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 4, ec_scheme="EC-3+1", 3, ec_scheme="EC-2+1", 2, 1=1, 1)
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-6+2", 2, ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 2, ec_scheme="EC-4+1" OR ec_scheme="EC-3+1" OR ec_scheme="EC-2+1", 1, 1=1, 0)
      | eval rf=if(ec_scheme="RF3", 3, if(ec_scheme="RF2", rf_raw, 0))
      | eval used_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(used_bytes, UsedBytes, used_bytes_total, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, usedLogicalGB, advertised_capacity_gb, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(used_bytes>0, used_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, usedPhysicalGB, "0"), 10))
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, rack_fault_domain_count, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h sourcetype="hyperflex:cluster:storage"
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, clusterUuid, cluster_uuid, hx_cluster_id)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, datastore_name, datastoreName, "hyperflex_cluster_storage")
      | eval vendor="cisco"
      | eval rf=tonumber(coalesce(replicationFactor, replication_factor, NumberOfDataCopies, "2"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme=if(rf=3, "RF3", "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=1
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=0
      | eval used_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(used_bytes, UsedBytes, used_capacity_bytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval total_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(total_bytes, TotalBytes, total_capacity_bytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=if(total_bytes>0 AND used_bytes>=0, total_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, "0"), 10))
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(used_bytes>0, used_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, "0"), 10))
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, node_count, nodeCount, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) earliest=-7d@h latest=@h ( sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:storage_pool" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:virtual_disk:resiliency" )
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, hci_cluster)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, FriendlyName, friendlyName, virtual_disk_name, VirtualDiskName, storage_pool_name, Name, "azure_hci_volume")
      | eval vendor="microsoft"
      | eval rsn=lower(tostring(coalesce(ResiliencySettingName, resiliency_setting_name, "Mirror")))
      | eval ndc=tonumber(coalesce(NumberOfDataCopies, number_of_data_copies, "2"), 10)
      | eval pdr=tonumber(coalesce(PhysicalDiskRedundancy, physical_disk_redundancy, "0"), 10)
      | eval ec_scheme=case(like(rsn,"%dual%parity%"), "EC-dual-parity", (like(rsn,"%single%parity%") OR (like(rsn,"%parity%") AND !like(rsn,"%mirror%") AND !like(rsn,"%map%"))), "EC-single-parity", like(rsn,"%mirror%accelerated%parity%") OR like(rsn,"%map%"), "MAP", like(rsn,"%mirror%"), if(ndc>=3, "RF3", "RF2"), 1=1, "RF2")
      | eval ec_data_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-dual-parity", pdr+2, ec_scheme="EC-single-parity", pdr+1, ec_scheme="MAP", 4, 1=1, 1)
      | eval ec_parity_blocks=case(ec_scheme="EC-dual-parity", 2, ec_scheme="EC-single-parity", 1, ec_scheme="MAP", 2, 1=1, 0)
      | eval rf=if(ec_scheme="RF3" OR ec_scheme="RF2", ndc, 0)
      | eval size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(Size, size_bytes, PoolSizeBytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval alloc_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(AllocatedSize, allocated_size_bytes, "0"), 10)
      | eval used_logical_gb=if(size_bytes>0, size_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_logical_gb, "0"), 10))
      | eval used_physical_gb=if(alloc_bytes>0, alloc_bytes/pow(1024,3), if(size_bytes>0, size_bytes/pow(1024,3), tonumber(coalesce(used_physical_gb, "0"), 10)))
      | eval fault_domain_count=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, OperationalNodes, node_count, "0"), 10)
      | bin span=1h _time
      | stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time ]
| eval cluster_id=if(isnotnull(cluster_id) AND len(tostring(cluster_id))>0, tostring(cluster_id), "UNKNOWN")
| eval container_id=if(isnotnull(container_id) AND len(tostring(container_id))>0, tostring(container_id), "UNKNOWN")
| where cluster_id!="UNKNOWN" AND container_id!="UNKNOWN" AND ( vendor="vmware" OR vendor="nutanix" OR vendor="cisco" OR vendor="microsoft" )
| eval capacity_overhead_expected_ratio=case(ec_scheme="RF1", 1.0, ec_scheme="RF2", 2.0, ec_scheme="RF3", 3.0, ec_scheme="EC-4+2", 1.5, ec_scheme="EC-4+1", 1.25, ec_scheme="EC-3+1", 4.0/3.0, ec_scheme="EC-2+1", 1.5, ec_scheme="EC-6+2", 8.0/6.0, ec_scheme="EC-single-parity", (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, ec_scheme="EC-dual-parity", (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, ec_scheme="MAP", (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1=1, if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 2.0))
| eval capacity_overhead_actual_ratio=if(used_logical_gb>0, used_physical_gb/used_logical_gb, null())
| eval capacity_drift_pct=if(isnotnull(capacity_overhead_expected_ratio) AND capacity_overhead_expected_ratio>0 AND isnotnull(capacity_overhead_actual_ratio), 100*((capacity_overhead_actual_ratio-capacity_overhead_expected_ratio)/capacity_overhead_expected_ratio), null())
| eventstats max(fault_domain_count) as fdom_peak by cluster_id, vendor, _time
| eval fault_domain_count=coalesce(fault_domain_count, fdom_peak)
| sort 0 cluster_id, container_id, vendor, _time
| streamstats window=168 global=f avg(capacity_drift_pct) as capacity_drift_pct_ma7 by cluster_id, container_id, vendor
| stats latest(ec_scheme) as ec_scheme latest(ec_data_blocks) as ec_data_blocks latest(ec_parity_blocks) as ec_parity_blocks latest(rf) as rf latest(used_logical_gb) as used_logical_gb latest(used_physical_gb) as used_physical_gb latest(fault_domain_count) as fault_domain_count latest(capacity_overhead_actual_ratio) as capacity_overhead_actual_ratio_join latest(capacity_overhead_expected_ratio) as capacity_overhead_expected_ratio_forjoin latest(capacity_drift_pct_ma7) as capacity_drift_pct max(_time) as _time by cluster_id, container_id, vendor
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id, vendor, container_id
    [| inputlookup hci_ec_policy_owner.csv
      | eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, CLUSTER_ID, cluster)
      | eval container_id=coalesce(container_id, CONTAINER_ID, container)
      | eval vendor=lower(tostring(vendor))
      | fields cluster_id, vendor, container_id, owner_team, target_max_capacity_drift_pct, target_max_rwaf, ec_target_scheme, change_freeze_until ]
| eval scheme_tgt=coalesce(ec_target_scheme, ec_scheme)
| eval capacity_overhead_expected_ratio=case(scheme_tgt="RF1", 1.0, scheme_tgt="RF2", 2.0, scheme_tgt="RF3", 3.0, scheme_tgt="EC-4+2", 1.5, scheme_tgt="EC-4+1", 1.25, scheme_tgt="EC-3+1", 4.0/3.0, scheme_tgt="EC-2+1", 1.5, scheme_tgt="EC-6+2", 8.0/6.0, scheme_tgt="EC-single-parity", if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1.25), scheme_tgt="EC-dual-parity", if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1.5), scheme_tgt="MAP", if(ec_data_blocks>0, (ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks)/ec_data_blocks, 1.5), 1=1, capacity_overhead_expected_ratio_forjoin)
| eval capacity_drift_pct=if(isnotnull(capacity_overhead_actual_ratio_join) AND isnotnull(capacity_overhead_expected_ratio) AND capacity_overhead_expected_ratio>0, 100*((capacity_overhead_actual_ratio_join-capacity_overhead_expected_ratio)/capacity_overhead_expected_ratio), capacity_drift_pct)
| fillnull value=5 target_max_capacity_drift_pct
| fillnull value=2.25 target_max_rwaf
| eval target_max_capacity_drift_pct=tonumber(target_max_capacity_drift_pct, 10)
| eval target_max_rwaf=tonumber(target_max_rwaf, 10)
| eval fault_domain_required=ec_data_blocks+ec_parity_blocks+1
| eval ec_misconfigured=if(fault_domain_count<fault_domain_required, 1, 0)
| eval ecdb=if(ec_data_blocks<1, 1, ec_data_blocks)
| eval rwaf_estimated=if(ec_scheme="RF2" OR ec_scheme="RF3" OR ec_scheme="RF1", 1.0, (ecdb+ec_parity_blocks)/ecdb)
| eval sev0=case(ec_misconfigured=1, "critical", rwaf_estimated>target_max_rwaf AND ec_parity_blocks>0, "high", ( abs(capacity_drift_pct)>target_max_capacity_drift_pct AND isnotnull(capacity_drift_pct) ), "warning", ec_misconfigured=0 AND rwaf_estimated<=target_max_rwaf AND ( isnull(capacity_drift_pct) OR abs(capacity_drift_pct)<=target_max_capacity_drift_pct ), "info", rwaf_estimated>target_max_rwaf, "high", 1=1, "warning")
| eval cft1=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(cft1) OR isnotnull(cft2) OR isnotnull(cft3) OR isnotnull(cft4), if(now()<coalesce(cft1,cft2,cft3,cft4,0),1,0),0)
| eval severity=if(freeze_active=1 AND sev0!="critical", "info", sev0)
| sort 0 - ec_misconfigured, - rwaf_estimated, cluster_id, container_id
| table vendor, cluster_id, container_id, ec_scheme, fault_domain_count, fault_domain_required, rwaf_estimated, capacity_drift_pct, severity, owner_team, change_freeze_until
```

## Visualization

Per-cluster EC scheme versus fault-domain gap scorecard; per-container rwaf_estimated table with conditional formatting over target_max_rwaf; capacity savings versus rebuild amplification scatter using overhead ratios; single value of ec_misconfigured count; timechart of smoothed capacity_drift_pct by vendor.

## Known False Positives

Cluster scale-out windows can leave fault_domain_count stale for one or two polls while hosts are cabled but not yet in the exported domain model; pair with freeze metadata before critical. Planned EC migrations from RF2 to EC-4+2 legitimately raise rwaf_estimated while capacity_overhead_actual_ratio improves; keep ec_target_scheme and target_max_rwaf aligned during the cutover week. Nutanix rack-aware clusters sometimes count racks that differ from node totals; reconcile UC-19.2.16 topology feeds before trusting a lone numeric gap. Azure Stack HCI mirror-accelerated parity tier rebalances during heavy random writes can swing used_physical_gb without any EC toggle. vSAN proactive rebalance after disk group replacement elevates component traffic and may nudge dedupe_compression_ratio in capacity feeds without an EC breach. Skyline calibration imports that lag by a day can make empirical bandwidth estimates look noisier than the pure RWAF model predicts.
